description_long: This is a small, single-story cabin with a simple rectangular footprint and a dark brown gable roof. The roof is steeply pitched and extends slightly beyond the walls, providing minimal overhang. The exterior walls are light-colored, likely finished in painted wood or siding, and the structure sits on a flat, circular base that could represent a yard or platform. There are no visible windows or doors on any side, giving the cabin a very minimal and utilitarian appearance. The roof appears to be made of shingles or tiles, contrasting with the lighter walls. The overall design is compact and functional, suitable for use as a small residential or utility building in an urban or park setting.
description: A compact, light-walled cabin with a dark brown gable roof and no visible windows or doors, set on a circular base. The design is minimal and functional.
description_view_0: The cabin is seen from above, showing the dark brown gable roof and the light-colored walls, with no visible openings.
description_view_1: This view highlights the gable end of the roof, with the triangular roof face and the light wall beneath, still no visible windows or doors.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, this angle shows the opposite side of the roof and walls, maintaining the same minimal appearance.
description_view_3: The other gable end is visible, again showing the triangular roof face and light wall, with no architectural features such as windows or doors.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate pitch and slight overhang, covered in dark shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: No windows are visible on any side of the cabin
attribute_door_placement: No exterior doors are visible on any side of the cabin
